front: Work: the title gaucho kills a black man in a knife fight and later has a singing contest (payada) with his brother
Work: the title gaucho befriends Sergeant Cruz; they go live among natives but are taken prisoner; Cruz dies of smallpox
Work: the protagonist of this poem is conscripted into the army after not voting for a judge
Work: in the famous payada, the law is compared to a spider's web that catches only small insects; an illiterate man can't answer a riddle about months with Rs
Work: Borges wrote 'The End' imagining the payada participants meeting again, and analyzed this poem in 'The Argentine Writer and Tradition'
Work: the gaucho proclaims 'I'm the bull in my own herd / and a braver bull in the next one' and 'Singing I'll die, singing they'll bury me'
Work: the son's tutor robbed him of inheritance left by his aunt in the second part of this poem
Work: this two-part gaucho epic is written in the verse structure of the payada, a rural ballad form
